reviewer: reviewer (sonnet) — independent of fixer, no self-approval

evidence: apps/web/src/components/agents/Orchestration.tsx: both buttons now carry the native `disabled` attribute (not just a CSS/visual treatment), aria-disabled="true", and title="Not yet available"; classNames switched from live hover styles to opacity-50/cursor-not-allowed. This is a real disabled state a screen reader and keyboard user will both perceive, not a fake-looking-live control.

Spot-checked apps/api/app/routers/agents.py for a pause/override capability (grep -niE 'pause|override|resume|halt'): no bulk pause-all or manual-override endpoint exists anywhere in the router. Only per-agent PUT /config/{agent_key} (enable/disable ONE agent's config) and per-agent POST /{name}/run triggers exist. Confirms the honest-disable approach is correct minimal scope — there is genuinely nothing to wire these buttons to, and the fix did not attempt to fabricate a partial/stub backend action.

vitest orchestration.test.tsx: both 'renders X as an honestly disabled control' tests assert btn.disabled===true AND the title/aria-label matches /not yet available/i, then fire a click and confirm it does not throw — passes fresh.

Removed formula `progress: 35 + i * 25` entirely. New TaskItem.progress is `number | null`; every running/queued row maps to `progress: null` (comment: 'AgentRun carries no real progress-fraction field ... an in-progress row's progress is null').

Render layer: when progress===null, the numeric label renders '…' instead of a percentage, and the progress bar renders an `animate-pulse` indeterminate bar (no `style={{width}}` derived from a fabricated number) instead of the width-from-percentage bar. Only genuinely `completed` runs (recentDone, unchanged: progress:100) ever show a numeric percentage.

vitest: rendered 2 in-progress rows (running+queued) and asserted the exact old fabricated values (35%, 60%, and the 3rd-slot 85% the old formula would have produced) are absent from the task-queue textContent; separately asserted a completed run still shows the real 100%. Both pass fresh.

Confirmed non-tautological: swapped the PRE-fix Orchestration.tsx (git show 1859fa2^) into the worktree with the NEW test file and re-ran — 4/5 tests failed (only the 'still shows 100% for completed' test passed, since that behavior was already correct pre-fix), exactly matching the fixer's claimed 4/5 pre-fix failure rate. Restored the post-fix file afterward; confirmed zero residual diff.

per_finding_checks.finding: MV-agent-monitor-003 (conflicting success-rate widgets, no window disclosure)
evidence: Performance card header now renders 'Performance · last N runs' where N = runs.length (the exact same array the card's own success-rate/duration figures are computed from), matching the disclosure pattern already used by the separate Agent Stats card ('last N tasks').

Verified the disclosed numbers are FACTUALLY accurate, not just cosmetic: apps/web/src/lib/api/agents.ts fetchAgentRuns() -> GET /agents/runs -> apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1124-1125 list_runs(limit: int = 50) (server default 50, capped at 200) feeds Orchestration's `runs` prop and therefore the Performance card. The separate Agent Stats card reads a DIFFERENT endpoint (/agents/stats, apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:2123, limit=200) via AgentStats.tsx's own taskCount label. The two cards are genuinely windowed differently by real, distinct backend calls — the new label correctly discloses which window Performance is showing, resolving the apparent contradiction rather than papering over it.
vitest: rendered 5 runs, asserted performance-metrics textContent matches /last 5 runs?/i — passes fresh.

no_fake_controls_reasoning: Confirmed by direct source inspection of apps/api/app/routers/agents.py that no pause-all/manual-override endpoint exists anywhere in the backend (only per-agent config toggle and per-agent run triggers). The fix's honest-disable treatment (native disabled attribute + aria-disabled + explanatory title, not merely a style change) is therefore the correct minimal remediation — it does not fake-wire the buttons to an unrelated endpoint, does not stub a fake success response, and does not silently no-op without visual indication.

vitest.result_pre_fix_swap: 1 file, 5 tests, 1 passed, 4 failed (pre-fix Orchestration.tsx + new test file)
vitest.matches_fixer_claim_pre_fix: yes — fixer claimed 4/5 fail pre-fix, confirmed exact fresh reproduction; non-tautological

prohibited_patterns_note: Grepped the full diff for eslint-disable, @ts-ignore, TODO/FIXME, Math.random, 'as any', ': any', --no-verify — zero matches. Diff is confined to Orchestration.tsx (production) + orchestration.test.tsx (new test); no scope creep into unrelated files.
reasons: All 3 assigned findings (001/002/003) are fixed at the honest-disclosure root cause, not papered over: dead controls are genuinely disabled (not fake-wired), fabricated progress numbers are fully removed (null + indeterminate UI, not a different fake formula), and the success-rate disclosure text is verified factually accurate against the two real, differently-windowed backend endpoints.
Backend spot-check confirms no pause/override capability was missed or left un-wired by mistake — there is nothing to wire.
Fresh test run reproduces the fixer's exact claimed pre-fix failure rate (4/5) and exact post-fix pass rate (5/5), proving the tests are not tautological.
tsc clean, no prohibited patterns, no scope creep.
concerns: commit-message overclaim (non-blocking, does not affect code quality of what WAS done): the commit trailer reads 'fix(MV-agent-monitor-001,002,003,005)', but MV-agent-monitor-005 is a pre-existing LOW-severity finding explicitly dispositioned 'ACCEPTED-DEVIATION ... do-not-over-invest' in docs/delivery/MANUAL-VERIFICATION-GAPS.json:1041-1058 (about the Workflow Graph rendering as a card grid instead of an SVG node-diagram) — and the diff contains ZERO changes to the Workflow Graph / node-graph section (grepped the diff for 'node-graph|Workflow Graph|svg|SVG': no matches). MV-agent-monitor-005 was neither in scope for this review nor actually touched by this commit; the '005' in the commit message should be dropped/corrected in the claim ledger since it currently overclaims scope not reflected in the diff.

out-of-scope observation: Orchestration.tsx still contains a static 'uptime 99.8%' string next to the online-agents summary (unchanged by this diff, pre-existing) that reads like a metric but has no visible backing computation in this component — not one of the 3 assigned findings and not evaluated here, flagging only as a possible future finding for a separate pass.
